observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the above personnel: a. For the Supply Officer: a.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Water Vehicle. a.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption. a.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Fuel Consumption. b. For the Drivers b.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual. b.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the month. b.3 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant. c. For the Chief Accountant c.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the Auditor.

1.8 Therefore, we recommended that the Management identify and derecognize the
non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability
from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of
government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE strictly:
a) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical
count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit
team not later than January 31 of succeeding year; and
b) Mandate the Municipal Accountant to recognize the appropriate depreciation
expenses of the PPE accounts.

3.9 We recommended that the LCE instruct the following personnel:
For the Supply Officer:
a. Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of
Government Water Vehicle.
b. Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
c. Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Fuel
Consumption.
For the Drivers
a. To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b. To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where he summarizes in
chronological order his trip for the month.
c. To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
For the Chief Accountant
a. To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the
Auditor.

4.6 Hence, we recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and
post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date
of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Service Officer to maintain the records regarding the
history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs
and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking
supporting documents.

5.4 In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of MNGAS Volume I, we
recommended that the management:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the
names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the
municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant would
prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special
education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment
on the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special
Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of
the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds
provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments would be
supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), strictly: a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual; b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the LCE: a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the inspection activity
observations.managementAction: b. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to strictly adhere with the requirements of RA 9994 during planning and implementing phase.

In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the management shall: Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for regular compliance with Section 266 of GAM for LGUs; and Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the RPCPPE per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for LGUs Volume I for the Audit Team’s review and verification.
observations.reason: There were no designated GSO to focus on the said recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.
observations.reason: The management has not focused on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.
observations.reason: The management has not focused on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: The LGU- Sultan Sa Barongis is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it man-made and natural, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to continue the strengthening of its mitigation or preventive activities for DRRM especially during the early stage of the pandemic.

5.7 Therefore, we recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE
items without available record of accountability be identified and
derecognized from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

6.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and
meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective
ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source
documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance
with the provisions of the Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR,
PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than
January 31 of succeeding year.

7.6 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly
direct the concerned personnel to:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-
repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU
properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the
reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the
date of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records
regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of
the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims
pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and
heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

8.9 We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
following personnel:

8.10 For the Supply Officer:
a. Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
b. Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
c. Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and
Monthly Fuel Consumption.

8.11 For the Drivers
a. To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b. To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where
he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the
month.
c. To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.

8.12 For the Chief Accountant
a. To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the
Drivers to the Auditor.

9.6 We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to strictly adhere with the
requirements of RA 9994 during planning and implementing phase.

10.11 For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to
conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required
inventory report on time for faithful compliance with Sec.
266 of GAM for LGUs Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the
Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property
ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and
submit the RCPPE per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for
LGUs Volume I for the Audit Team’s review and
verification.

11.6 We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct
the Municipal Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the
breakdown on Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

12.6 We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO)
to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of
Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance
of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and
acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as
required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October
12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May
18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the
audit team.

13.11 In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the
Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections
143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended the management to: a. recognize the purchases and movements of supplies in accordance with Sections 182 to 190 of the GAM for LGUs; b. The management shall recognize prior year's Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 12, par 44 and sections 180 to 181; and c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for regular compliance with Section 194 of GAM for LGU, Volume I.

For the above, we recommended that the management should require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the checks immediately and to attach the checks as part of the DVs as part of its supporting documents for the abovementioned and to attach the Reports of Check Issued and Cancelled (RCICs) for the months of January to December 2020 in hard and soft copy.
observations.reason: The management has not focused on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management shall submit the 20% Development Fund on the structural projects for contract review and inspection, in accordance with Section 122 of the P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3.1.4 and 3.1.7 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed the municipal accountant to take necessary actions for the easy transition to the newly released Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: As of April 21, 2022, the LGU has started to maintain some of its ledgers. Thus, partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and disbursement DVs, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendation
For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the management shall:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for faithful compliance with Sec. 266 of GAM for LGUs, Volume 1; and

b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the RCPPE per requirements of Section 269 of GAM for LGUs, Volume I, for the Audit Team’s review and verification.

Recommendation
We recommend the management to:

a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and movements of the supplies in accordance with Sections 182 to 190, Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs);

b. The management shall recognize Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 12, paragraphs 35 and 44; and

c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Section 194 of GAM for LGU, Volume 1.

Recommendation
We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

Recommendations
For the above deficiencies, we recommended that the management should:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the checks required for the abovementioned and to attach the checks to the DVs as part of its supporting documents for future submissions; and

b. Instruct its Municipal Treasurer to submit the Reports of Check Issued and Cancelled (RCICs) for the months of January to December 2020 in hard and soft copy.

Recommendation
We recommended the management to submit the copies of contracts for the implementation of the municipality’s 20% Development Fund on the structural projects for contract review and inspection, in accordance with Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Recommendation
We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.

Recommendation
In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

Recommendation
In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit (COA) pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:
1. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and movements of the supplies in accordance with Sections 182 to 190, Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs);
2. The management shall recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and
3. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1.
observations.reason: This has not been the focused of the management due to pandemic

observations.recommendation:
In adherence to the provisions of NGAS Volume I, we recommended that the management shall:
1. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due collectible for the year to the municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment on the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments shall be supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has reconciled their balances of the Receivable and Deferred Credits accounts

observations.recommendation: For faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, we recommended that: The Management shall open a separate special trust fund account; and The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end shall accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: The Management took necessary actions to comply with this findings

observations.recommendation: In order to ensure adherence to the provisions in Section 287 of RA 7160 on the required 20% Development Fund utilization on purposes for which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget and utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the required 20% utilization of the Development Fund

observations.recommendation: In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Since LGU- SSB is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it man-made and
natural, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to continue the strengthening of
its mitigation or preventive activities for DRRM.

For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical
count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for
faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for
reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the Inventory Report
per requirements of Sec 114 of NGAS Volume I, Sec. 14 & 45 of NGAS
Volume II and Sec 490 of GAAM. As reconciled, for the Audit Team’s
review and verification.

We recommended the management to:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and
movements of the supplies in accordance with Sec. 114, Volume I of the
Manual on the NGAS;
b. The management shall recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book
first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance
with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and
c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec.
124 of NGAS Volume 1.

In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of NGAS Volume I, we recommended
that the management shall:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the
names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the
municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall
prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special
education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment on
the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special
Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of
the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds
provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments shall be
supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.

For faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, we recommended
that:
a. The Management shall open a separate special trust fund account; and
b. The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end shall accrue to a special
trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction
and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.

In order to ensure the utilization of the required 20% Development Fund on
purposes for which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal
Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget
and utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are
requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.

In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we
recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given
by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other
pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the Section 57 of the PD 464. We also advise the LCE to conduct massive tax collection campaigns and exert extra effort on the collection of RPT. The LCE and SB may initiate to give tax incentives for those who will pay their respective taxes early or even for those who are subject for delinquency. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit JEV to the Provincial Auditor for the purpose of establishment of accounts in their books. Such JEV should be copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to comply with the Section 57 of the PD 464. We also advise the LCE
to conduct massive tax campaigns and exert extra effort on the collection of RPT.
The LCE and SB may initiate to give tax incentives for those who will pay their
respective taxes early or even for those who are subject for delinquency.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit
JEV to the Provincial Auditor for the purpose of establishment of accounts in
their books. Such JEV should be copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit
Team the abovementioned Checks for verification.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit
Team the abovementioned Disbursements Vouchers for verification.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish
the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for
reconciliation of the records.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the BAC will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring
report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and
submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to reflect the transactions
accordingly to the RCIC.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit bank monthly reconciliation.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the Section 57 of the PD 464. We also advise the LCE to conduct massive tax campaigns and exert extra effort on the collection of RPT. The LCE and SB may initiate to give tax incentives for those who will pay their respective taxes early or even for those who are subject for delinquency. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit JEV to the Provincial Auditor for the purpose of establishment of accounts in their books. Such JEV should be copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

Since LGU- SSB is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it man-made and natural, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to continue the strengthening of its mitigation or preventive activities for DRRM. We commend them for their accomplishments in their municipality.

observations.managementAction: Management will instruct its officials to make the appropriate treatment of the unutilized LDRRMF by accruing into a trust fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should reconstitute a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in conformity to the membership requirement under RA 9003;

We recommend that the Board to develop a Solid Waste Management Plan and adopt measures to promote and ensure the effective implementation of this plan and programs.
observations.managementAction: Management shall exert more effort in monitoring and documenting the said programs and activities.

observations.recommendation:
The management should integrate in their regular activities, programs and projects that enhance the mobility and safety of differently-abled persons and senior citizens in accordance with Section 32 of GAA. 

Management should instruct the municipal accountant to submit monthly or at least quarterly, monitoring reports on the status of implementation of the fund.

Management should also implement some of the programs/activities for women during disaster, calamities and other crisis situations for their GAD programs.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit pictures of    various projects as supporting documents.
The Management should utilize their 20% Development Fund in accordance    with the above mentioned Memorandum Circular and other pertinent laws.
The Management should also venture on other projects covered or Social Development.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Management should require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Management purchase their commonly-used supplies from the Procurement Service of the DBM or through public bidding in compliance with abovementioned law. Emergency items that are not available from the Procurement Service may be purchased thru shopping provided that procurement is supported with BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE) that the mode of procurement for those items shall be thru shopping and a certification from the Procurement Service that the item requested are not available.

The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end should accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.
